title: Here's Why Investors Should Avoid Norfolk Southern (NSC) Now
text: Low revenues, supply-chain woes and high labor costs are hurting Norfolk Southern's (NSC) prospects.

title: Why Is Norfolk Southern (NSC) Up 15.4% Since Last Earnings Report?
text: Norfolk Southern (NSC) reported earnings 30 days ago. What's next for the stock?

title: Norfolk Southern: All Aboard This Buy-And-Hold Freight Train
text: Norfolk Southern is a Class I railroad operator in the U.S. with a wide range of revenue streams in the transport of intermodal freight and other cargo. The company has a wide economic moat due to its large rail network and established market position in the Eastern United States. Norfolk Southern has historically shown strong financial performance along with a newfound desire for operational excellence.

title: Norfolk Southern: All Aboard This Superb Dividend Growth Stock
text: Alongside trucking companies, railroad operators form the backbone of what keeps U.S. logistics running smoothly. A softening economy and the tragic East Palestine, Ohio incident weighed on Norfolk Southern's third-quarter operating results. The railway operator possesses a BBB+ credit rating from S&P on a stable outlook.

title: 3 Dividend Stocks with Strong Growth Potential in Q4
text: If this is the beginning of a broader market rally, many short-term investors may shift away from dividend stocks. After a year or more of playing defense, it makes sense that risk-tolerant traders and investors will want to consider equities with more upside.

title: Norfolk Southern to present at the Stephens Annual Investment Conference

title: What's Next For Norfolk Southern Stock After A 21% Fall This Year?
text: Norfolk Southern (NYSE: NSC) reported its Q3 results last month, with revenues meeting but earnings missing the street estimates.
